Task Name: Freeze frontend-consumed API response contracts
Description content: Document and freeze the exact response shapes for student enrollments, progress, streaks, and active bug-fix endpoints so frontend work can proceed without guessing. This removes silent rendering bugs caused by inconsistent payloads.
Subtasks: Audit current response shapes|Standardize final route shape|Publish success empty and error payloads|Share contract note with Jimmy and Mohamed|Provide stub payloads for unfinished routes
Dependencies: None — can be done in parallel
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Each targeted endpoint has a documented final response shape|[ ] Each route includes at least one sample payload|[ ] Empty-state and error-state payloads are defined|[ ] Jimmy can implement pages without waiting for live backend changes
Technical Notes: APIs: /api/enrollments/student, /api/progress/overall, /api/progress/courses, /api/streaks/me. Modules: server/src/api/enrollment.routes.ts, server/src/api/progress.routes.ts. If final implementation is not ready, provide stub JSON with the final contract.

Task Name: Freeze gamification API and shared type contracts
Description content: Lock the gamification payload shapes for profile, badges, leaderboard, activity, admin settings, rules, and reports so frontend and testing work can proceed independently.
Subtasks: Review current gamification routes and shared types|Finalize response shapes in shared types|Publish student and admin sample payloads|Define query keys and cache invalidation notes|Share contract pack with Jimmy and QA
Dependencies: None — can be done in parallel
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Shared gamification types reflect the intended API shape|[ ] Student and admin gamification sample payloads exist|[ ] Query keys and refresh behavior are documented|[ ] Jimmy can render gamification UI from samples without backend dependency
Technical Notes: APIs: /api/gamification/me, /api/gamification/me/badges, /api/gamification/leaderboard, /api/gamification/activity, /api/admin/gamification/*. Files: shared/gamification.types.ts and gamification routes/services.

Task Name: Define security error and middleware contract
Description content: Create a stable contract for auth failures, rate-limit responses, and high-risk middleware behavior so backend and frontend teams know exactly how protected routes will behave this week.
Subtasks: Audit auth session and rate-limit middleware behavior|Define standard error response shape|List top-risk routes following this contract|Publish local developer testing notes|Share with Zeyad Jimmy and Youssef
Dependencies: None — can be done in parallel
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Security error response shape is documented|[ ] Protected route categories are listed|[ ] Local developer testing guidance is available|[ ] No team member is blocked by unclear auth behavior
Technical Notes: Modules: server/src/index.ts, server/src/middleware/*. Avoid changing feature contracts while defining security behavior.

Task Name: Prepare frontend fixture adapters for unstable APIs
Description content: Set up local mock and fixture support so frontend bug-fix and gamification UI work can proceed even if backend routes are incomplete or unstable.
Subtasks: Create fixture payloads for enrollments progress streaks gamification and AI|Add helper to switch between live and fixture data|Validate fixture rendering on student pages|Document how to toggle fixture mode|Flag contract mismatches immediately
Dependencies: Depends on T-001 and T-002 contracts when available; otherwise use current best-known shapes and update later
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Student courses page renders from fixture data|[ ] Student dashboard renders from fixture data|[ ] Student gamification page renders from fixture data|[ ] Fixture mode can be enabled without editing multiple files
Technical Notes: Files: client/src/pages/* and lightweight mock helpers under client/src. Do not edit gamification-owned components without Mohamed’s contract signoff.

Task Name: Define AI orchestration contract and stub executor
Description content: Freeze the request and response schema for the AI workflow orchestrator and provide a deterministic stub implementation so UI and integration work can continue before live AI logic is complete.
Subtasks: Define orchestration request payload|Define orchestration response with fallback and trace|Create sample request and response JSON|Define deterministic stub outputs|Share contract with Jimmy and Mohamed
Dependencies: None — can be done in parallel
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] AI orchestration request schema is documented|[ ] AI orchestration response schema is documented|[ ] Stub responses exist for success and fallback scenarios|[ ] Frontend can build against the stub without waiting for AI backend completion
Technical Notes: Target API: POST /api/ai-orchestra/execute. Constraint: one stable schema only and no midweek contract drift.

Task Name: Fix student courses page data parsing and rendering stability
Description content: Fix the student courses page so it correctly renders paginated enrollment responses and never silently displays an empty list for valid data.
Subtasks: Audit current parsing logic|Support paginated data response shape|Add explicit error and empty-state UI|Validate with fixture and live responses|Record before and after screenshots
Dependencies: None — can be done in parallel with live backend fixes using fixtures
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Courses render correctly when API returns paginated data|[ ] Real empty enrollment state shows intentional messaging|[ ] API error shows intentional error state|[ ] No silent blank list remains on the page
Technical Notes: Files: client/src/pages/student-courses.tsx and any shared student hooks if needed. If live route remains unstable, keep fixture adapter until Zeyad confirms contract.

Task Name: Fix student dashboard data aggregation and fallback states
Description content: Stabilize student dashboard rendering across enrollments, progress, and streak responses so the page remains usable even when one source is empty or temporarily unavailable.
Subtasks: Audit current dashboard fetch and parsing logic|Normalize response handling|Add empty loading and partial-data states|Validate widgets with live and fixture payloads|Document remaining backend mismatches
Dependencies: None — can be done in parallel with fixtures
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Dashboard loads with full data when all endpoints succeed|[ ] Dashboard still renders partial UI when one endpoint fails|[ ] Empty state messaging is clear and intentional|[ ] No silent render failures remain
Technical Notes: Files: client/src/pages/student-dashboard.tsx, client/src/hooks/useStudentDashboard.ts. APIs: enrollments progress streak routes.

Task Name: Normalize student and teacher API route shapes
Description content: Fix backend route inconsistencies causing frontend bugs, especially array versus object drift, missing wrappers, and incompatible empty-state behavior.
Subtasks: Identify top impacted routes|Normalize response wrappers and pagination metadata|Ensure status codes and error payloads are consistent|Verify affected consumers|Publish route changelog
Dependencies: None — can be done in parallel
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Targeted routes return consistent documented shapes|[ ] Empty responses are explicit and predictable|[ ] Error responses use standard structure|[ ] Frontend consumers can parse routes without custom hacks per page
Technical Notes: Files: selected server/src/api/*.ts excluding gamification and security-owned routes. Do not change gamification or security routes without coordination.

Task Name: Complete persistent lesson completion event flow
Description content: Ensure lesson completion is durably recorded in the database and survives refresh even when points are disabled. This is the critical path for making gamification reliable.
Subtasks: Audit lesson completion endpoint to event persistence|Ensure completion event is written even when points are zero|Verify activity endpoint restores completed lessons|Validate course completion behavior on last lesson|Document event persistence rules
Dependencies: None — can be done in parallel
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Completing a lesson creates a durable completion record|[ ] Refreshing the lesson viewer shows completed lessons correctly|[ ] Duplicate completion remains idempotent|[ ] Course completion fires only under valid final-lesson conditions
Technical Notes: Files: lesson completion route, gamification service, activity route, lesson viewer consumer. Completion persistence must not depend solely on points being enabled.

Task Name: Complete student gamification profile refresh behavior
Description content: Make the student gamification experience update immediately after completions and load correctly from persisted state on revisit.
Subtasks: Review query invalidation and refresh behavior|Ensure profile badges and activity feed update|Validate empty disabled and active states|Verify route mounting and auth behavior|Produce UI verification checklist
Dependencies: Depends on T-009 preferred but can be mocked in parallel
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Student gamification page updates after completion actions|[ ] Refreshing the page shows persisted state|[ ] Empty and disabled states are accurate|[ ] No endpoint returns 404 for mounted gamification routes
Technical Notes: Files: gamification pages hooks components and route mounting. Use shared gamification types as source of truth.

Task Name: Implement admin gamification settings and rules stability pass
Description content: Stabilize the admin-side gamification controls so the platform can safely configure points, naming, badges, and feature flags during integration.
Subtasks: Validate settings get and put flow|Validate default rule seeding behavior|Verify rules update validation and persistence|Validate admin badges list create and update contract|Document admin-side usage and constraints
Dependencies: None — can be done in parallel
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Admin can fetch and update gamification settings|[ ] Default rules exist or are safely seeded|[ ] Invalid rule updates are rejected predictably|[ ] Badge CRUD contract is usable by frontend consumers
Technical Notes: Files: server/src/api/admin-gamification.routes.ts, gamification service, admin hooks if needed. No frontend polish beyond what is needed to validate contract.

Task Name: Harden rate limiting and sensitive middleware behavior
Description content: Improve baseline protection on sensitive routes, especially auth, AI, uploads, and high-risk POST endpoints, without disrupting normal developer flow.
Subtasks: Audit current rate-limit configuration and key generation|Apply safe coverage to sensitive endpoints|Standardize middleware behavior and error payloads|Validate local ergonomics and proxy IP handling|Document route coverage and thresholds
Dependencies: None — can be done in parallel
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Sensitive routes are protected by appropriate rate limiting|[ ] IPv4 and IPv6 and proxy-safe key behavior is correct|[ ] Error responses follow the documented security contract|[ ] Local development still works with documented expectations
Technical Notes: Files: server/src/index.ts and middleware files. Avoid breaking legitimate QA and dev workflows.

Task Name: Harden upload anti-cheat and restricted endpoint protections
Description content: Ensure upload and proctoring-related endpoints enforce role checks, input validation, and safe error behavior because these are high-risk surfaces.
Subtasks: Audit recording upload and anti-cheat routes|Validate role-based access on view and write actions|Verify file size and type restrictions|Validate suspicious activity logging|Publish risk findings and remediations
Dependencies: None — can be done in parallel
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Unauthorized users cannot access protected upload and proctoring endpoints|[ ] Upload validation rejects invalid files safely|[ ] High-risk route failures are logged consistently|[ ] Risk register captures remaining issues clearly
Technical Notes: Files: server/src/api/anti-cheat.routes.ts, server/src/api/recording-upload.routes.ts, related middleware and services.

Task Name: Build AI orchestration service shell
Description content: Implement the orchestration service entrypoint that can receive a structured task, route it through the intended workflow, and return a traceable response shape.
Subtasks: Create orchestration service module and route scaffold|Parse and validate request payload|Implement stub-to-live routing structure|Return deterministic response with trace metadata|Document execution flow
Dependencies: None — can be done in parallel
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Orchestration endpoint exists and accepts the frozen request schema|[ ] Endpoint returns valid structured responses|[ ] Trace metadata is included|[ ] Service can run in stub mode without external AI dependencies
Technical Notes: Files: AI routes and services. Constraint: one working orchestrated path this week is enough and overbuilding should be avoided.

Task Name: Implement one real AI orchestration workflow with fallback
Description content: Deliver one functioning workflow through the orchestrator using existing lesson retrieval or chat capability where possible and a graceful fallback when AI infra is unavailable.
Subtasks: Select one workflow path|Integrate existing lesson retrieval or AI chat service|Add fallback path for missing key or unavailable vector DB|Verify response consistency across success and fallback|Document operational dependencies
Dependencies: Depends on T-014
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] One orchestrated workflow works end to end|[ ] Missing AI provider or retrieval infra returns graceful fallback|[ ] Response schema stays identical in success and fallback modes|[ ] Operational dependencies are documented
Technical Notes: Files: server/src/api/ai-chat.routes.ts, server/src/services/ai.service.ts, server/src/services/lesson-digestion.service.ts, orchestration service.

Task Name: Replace frontend mocks with live backend routes where ready
Description content: Swap fixture-backed flows to live APIs for completed modules and verify the UI behaves the same with real data.
Subtasks: Identify routes ready for live integration|Toggle pages and hooks from fixtures to live mode|Validate all state modes with live data|Record integration issues and hand back to owners|Keep unresolved flows on fixture mode with explicit flag
Dependencies: Depends on T-008, T-010, and T-015 where relevant; if not ready keep mocks and document carry-over
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Live-integrated pages behave the same as fixture-backed versions|[ ] Broken live flows are documented with owner and route|[ ] No unresolved integration issue silently blocks the UI|[ ] Remaining mock-backed flows are clearly marked for carry-over
Technical Notes: Do not wait on unfinished routes. Keep fixture fallback where needed.

Task Name: Run backend contract verification sweep
Description content: Verify all changed backend routes against their documented contracts and catch mismatches before final testing day.
Subtasks: Re-run target route requests using sample payload scenarios|Compare live responses to documented schema|Fix drift or document exceptions|Coordinate with Mohamed for gamification route checks|Publish backend verification summary
Dependencies: Depends on core backend and gamification route work being complete enough for validation
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] All changed routes are checked against documented schemas|[ ] Contract mismatches are fixed or explicitly documented|[ ] Status codes are verified|[ ] Backend verification summary is published
Technical Notes: Use curl or Postman or equivalent smoke tooling. No new scope should be added during verification.

Task Name: Run security regression sweep on changed endpoints
Description content: Validate that this week’s changed or added routes still meet baseline security expectations after integration.
Subtasks: Re-test protected endpoints with invalid auth and role|Re-test rate-limited endpoints|Re-test upload and AI-related sensitive routes|Log any high-severity findings|Publish regression summary and residual risks
Dependencies: Depends on core implementation tasks being complete enough for validation
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Protected endpoints reject unauthorized requests correctly|[ ] Sensitive endpoints respect new middleware behavior|[ ] Any residual security issues are severity-tagged|[ ] Regression summary is published
Technical Notes: Coordinate with Youssef on AI endpoint behavior and Zeyad on generic routes.

Task Name: Run gamification integration checkpoint
Description content: Verify the full gamification slice together across backend, frontend, and admin configuration so final testing is based on a working end-to-end path.
Subtasks: Validate lesson completion to event persistence|Validate student profile and activity refresh|Validate admin settings rules and badges flows|Validate leaderboard and report state behavior|Capture known issues and assign owners immediately
Dependencies: Depends on T-009, T-010, T-011, and Jimmy’s related frontend integration
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Student completion updates gamification consistently|[ ] Refresh preserves gamification-related state|[ ] Admin flows are testable end to end|[ ] Known issues are logged with owner and severity
Technical Notes: No unresolved issue leaves the checkpoint without owner or workaround.

Task Name: Run AI orchestration integration checkpoint
Description content: Validate the orchestrator in a realistic environment with auth, lesson context if available, and failure simulation.
Subtasks: Test authenticated orchestration request|Test lesson-context path if available|Test fallback path with disabled AI dependency|Validate trace metadata and output shape|Publish integration summary
Dependencies: Depends on T-014 and T-015; frontend consumer is optional
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Orchestrator works with authenticated request|[ ] Success path returns expected structured response|[ ] Failure path returns graceful fallback|[ ] Trace metadata is visible and useful
Technical Notes: Backend-first validation is acceptable this week.

Task Name: Final frontend regression pass and evidence collection
Description content: Run a final regression sweep across all touched UI flows and produce evidence for what was fixed and what still needs follow-up.
Subtasks: Re-test student pages touched this week|Re-test teacher and admin pages touched this week|Capture screenshots or short videos|Update frontend bug-fix doc|Log carry-over issues clearly
Dependencies: Depends on Day 4 integration tasks
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Every touched UI route has a tested outcome|[ ] Evidence exists for fixed flows|[ ] Remaining issues are documented clearly|[ ] No silent blank-state bug remains in tested areas
Technical Notes: Documentation target: docs/frontend-weekly-bugfixes.md. Use a junior-friendly checklist format.

Task Name: Final backend smoke run and route changelog
Description content: Perform final backend smoke validation and publish a concise changelog of fixed routes, payload changes, and known issues.
Subtasks: Run final route smoke tests|Confirm key routes return documented shapes|Compile changed-route summary|Flag unresolved backend issues|Share final handoff note
Dependencies: Depends on Day 4 verification complete
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Smoke-tested routes pass expected checks|[ ] Changelog includes route change and risk|[ ] Known unresolved issues are explicit|[ ] Final note is ready for lead review

Task Name: Final security signoff and risk register update
Description content: Provide final security review for this sprint’s changes and update the risk register with anything that remains open.

Task Name: Final AI runbook and demo-ready validation
Description content: Finalize documentation and validation for the AI orchestration slice so it can be demonstrated and handed off cleanly.
Subtasks: Validate final success and fallback responses|Finalize environment requirements|Finalize runbook and troubleshooting notes|Capture one demo scenario|Log next-step improvements for future sprint

Task Name: Final cross-team integration review and sprint closeout
Description content: Run the final cross-team review, ensure every module has evidence and documentation, and produce the sprint closeout summary.
Subtasks: Collect final validation summaries from all owners|Review unresolved issues and decide keep fix or escalate|Produce sprint closeout note|Confirm demo-ready flows|Confirm next sprint carry-over list

Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Every module owner submits final status and evidence|[ ] Carry-over issues have owner and reason|[ ] Sprint closeout note is complete|[ ] Demo-ready flows are identified clearly
